'''Machineville''' was a district in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]' [[Lower City]]. Set aside for the [[City/Legends|city's]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] population as a place to recharge and repair, Machineville was known as the "birthplace of the [[Swoop/Legends|swoop-bike]]" among Taris' [[Swoop racing/Legends|racing]] community. Machineville was located near a number of [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]] camps set up during the [[Mandalorian Wars]], it and served as a buffer zone between these settlements and the rest of the Lower City.

==Description==
Machineville was an area located in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]' [[Lower City]] during the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]].<ref name="kotor3" /> True to its name, Machineville was set aside for the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] population of the Lower [[City/Legends|City]], as a place where they could recharge and repair themselves. The area also served as a buffer zone between the rest of the Lower City and [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]] camps set up during the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] Wars.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|II|273|Machineville}}</ref> Machineville was also located near a historic [[Lhosan Industries]] [[Unidentified Lhosan Industries plant|plant]],<ref name="siege">{{KOTORbackups|15}}</ref> along with the desolate [[Junk Junction]] area of the Lower City.<ref name="kotor3" />

==History==
In the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the Mandalorian Wars, many refugees began to settle and form camps near Machineville,<ref name="cswe" /> which was known as the "birthplace of the [[Swoop/Legends|swoop-bike]]" among the Tarisian [[Swoop racing/Legends|racing]] community.<ref name="siege" /> Eventually, Machineville began to be regarded as a buffer zone between these camps and the rest of the Lower City.<ref name="cswe" /> In [[3963 BBY]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] [[Taris Siege|invaded]] Taris itself and laid waste to much of its cityscape. Thousands of Tarisians took shelter in the recently abandoned Lhosan Industries plant near Machineville, only for the Mandalorians to destroy their newfound sanctuary. [[Death/Legends|Loss of life]] was reported to be high.<ref name="siege" />

==Behind the scenes==
Machineville was first mentioned in [[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 3]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]]<ref name="kotor3" /> and released on [[March 29]], [[2006]].<ref name="DH">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-878/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-3|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #3}}</ref>
